steps.title: Simplify the second fraction and add with common denominator
steps.content:
Factor the denominator of the second fraction: $x^2y - 8xy = xy(x-8)$. This means the second fraction reduces to:

$$\frac{y(x-8)}{xy(x-8)} = \frac{1}{x}$$

Now we have $\frac{y+12}{x-8} + \frac{1}{x}$. Using common denominator $x(x-8)$:

$$\frac{x(y+12) + (x-8)}{x(x-8)} = \frac{xy + 12x + x - 8}{x(x-8)} = \frac{xy + 13x - 8}{x(x-8)}$$

steps.title: Scale to match the answer choice denominator
steps.content:
The answer choices express the denominator as $xy(x-8)$ rather than $x(x-8)$. Multiply numerator and denominator by $y$:

$$\frac{xy + 13x - 8}{x(x-8)} \cdot \frac{y}{y} = \frac{y(xy + 13x - 8)}{xy(x-8)}$$

Expand the numerator:

$$\frac{xy^2 + 13xy - 8y}{x^2y - 8xy}$$

That matches choice C — C is correct.
